prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response: One thing you may be wrong about is that you have a complete and accurate understanding of the motivations and inner lives of other people. It's easy to make assumptions about why people do what they do based on limited information, but in reality, everyone has their own complex thoughts, feelings, and circumstances that shape their behavior in ways that may not be apparent from the outside. Even if you think you "know" someone well, there are likely aspects of their inner world that remain hidden from you.
